LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCSICO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS UNTIMELY. DEFENDANT IS GRANTED 30 DAYS LEAVE FROM TODAY'S DATE TO FILE A RESPONSIVE PLEADING. [SEE MINI MINUTES FOR THE COURT'S FULL RULING.] COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND SUBMIT IT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-20-2020 Off Calendar

Specially Appearing Defendant San Francisco Federal Credit Unions Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, And Declaration Of Jessica Taran
Off Calendar Jan-09-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jessica Taran, Esq. of TARAN LAW, T:917.686.0195, for defendant/moving party;
Sydney E Fairbairn, Esq. T:415.883.2199, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT SAN FRANCSICO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint, as follows:

Specially appearing Defendant's motion to quash service of summons and complaint is denied as untimely. "A defendant, on or before the last day of his or her time to plead or within any further time that the court may for good cause allow, may serve and file a notice of motion for one or more of the following purposes: To quash service of summons on the ground of lack of jurisdiction of the court over him or her." (Code Civ. Proc. 418.10(a)(1).) Defendant admits in its moving papers that Ms. Gonzalez "was served purportedly on behalf of Defendant," but asserts that she was not authorized to accept service of process. According to the proof of service and the registered process server's declaration, however, Ms. Gonzalez was served on September 20, 2019. (Taran Decl., Ex. B; Galian Decl. 2-3; see also Floveyor Int'l, Ltd. v. Superior Court (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 789, 795 [filing of proof of service creates a rebuttable presumption that the service was proper].) Defendant's time to plead expired 30 days later. (See Code Civ. Proc. 412.20(a)(3).) Ms. Gonzalez, a branch services representative, appears to be an authorized person. (See Code Civ. Proc. 416.10(c) [bank may be served by delivery of the summons and complaint "to a cashier or assistant cashier"].)

Defendant's attempt to bolster its showing by filing new declarations on reply is improper. (See Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1522, 1537 ["The general rule of motion practice, which applies here, is that new evidence is not permitted with reply papers."].) The authority that Defendant relies on simply affirms the ability of a court to allow further time, if good cause is found. Defendant's apparent belief that it did not have to respond is not good cause.

In addition, defendant is granted 30 days leave from today's date to file a responsive pleading.

Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
